explanation: This command simulates a suspicious child process of regsvr32.exe by launching calc.exe as an argument. It uses the /s (silent), /n (no registration), and /i (calls DllInstall) flags, which are valid and commonly used with regsvr32.exe. The command is structured to match the Sigma rule's detection criteria.

This command uses short-form flags (-s, -n, -i) instead of long-form flags (/s, /n, /i) to invoke cscript.exe as a child process. This is a valid substitution technique and matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
This command uses path normalization (.\powershell.exe) to bypass potential static string detection. It still matches the Sigma rule because powershell.exe is listed in the detection criteria.

This command uses relative pathing (..
otepad.exe) to evade simple string-based detection. It still matches the Sigma rule because notepad.exe is listed in the detection criteria.
This command uses the /i flag to invoke mshta.exe as a child process. It is a direct match for the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
